title: Project Report: FastAPI REST + PostgreSQL Blog API — Python Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Async FastAPI blog with SQLAlchemy 2.0, JWT auth, Alembic migrations, and Docker deployment.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Intermediate)*

**FastAPI REST + PostgreSQL Blog API** is a intermediate-level end-to-end project for Python Developer. Duration: 3–4 weeks. Async FastAPI blog with SQLAlchemy 2.0, JWT auth, Alembic migrations, and Docker deployment. Tools: FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, Docker.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Async SQLAlchemy sessions
Objective: JWT login/register
Objective: CRUD posts with author ownership
Objective: Deploy on Railway/Docker
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Python Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Python Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Python Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Models
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Models** *(Level: Intermediate)*

User and Post SQLAlchemy models. Complete these tasks: Alembic migration; Relationships. Deliverable for this phase: Migrated schema.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Alembic migration
- [ ] Relationships
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Models is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Python Developer agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Migrated schema to the teacher.

chapters.title: Phase: Auth
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Auth** *(Level: Intermediate)*

OAuth2 password flow. Complete these tasks: JWT tokens; Protected routes. Deliverable for this phase: Auth Postman tests.

Code Reference:

```text
- [ ] JWT tokens
- [ ] Protected routes
```

chapters.title: Phase: API
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: API** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Post CRUD with pagination. Complete these tasks: Pydantic v2 schemas; OpenAPI docs. Deliverable for this phase: /docs working.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Pydantic v2 schemas
- [ ] OpenAPI docs
```

chapters.title: Phase: Deploy
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Deploy** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Docker + CI. Complete these tasks: GitHub Actions; Railway deploy. Deliverable for this phase: Live URL.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] GitHub Actions
- [ ] Railway deploy
```

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Submit all final deliverables: Live API URL; OpenAPI spec; CI badge; Architecture README. Skills demonstrated: FastAPI, Async, Auth, ORM. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Live API URL
Deliverable: OpenAPI spec
Deliverable: CI badge
Deliverable: Architecture README
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Live API URL and passes the rubric.
